description_long: This is a vintage, single-door refrigerator with a boxy, rectangular form and rounded edges, finished in a faded beige color with visible patches of rust and wear, suggesting age and heavy use. The front features a metallic handle on the upper section and two horizontal lower drawers, both slightly recessed. The sides are plain, showing more signs of rust and discoloration, while the back is dominated by a large vented grille with horizontal slats and a lower access panel, as well as a visible power cord with a red plug. The overall design is utilitarian, with no visible branding or digital controls, and the object appears to be constructed primarily from metal with some plastic and rubber components. The refrigerator is of a size typical for older household models, likely standing just under chest height for an adult. Its weathered appearance makes it suitable for urban environments such as alleys, garages, or abandoned lots.
description: A weathered, vintage beige refrigerator with a metallic handle, two lower drawers, and a large vented grille on the back. Rust patches and a visible power cord add to its aged, utilitarian look.
description_view_0: Front view shows the main door with a metallic handle and two lower drawers, with visible rust patches.
description_view_1: Left side view reveals plain, slightly rounded paneling with rust stains and the power cord emerging from the back.
description_view_2: Rear view displays a large vented grille with horizontal slats, a lower access panel, and the power cord with a red plug.
description_view_3: Right side view is similar to the left, showing more rust patches and the edge of the handle.
attribute_door_handle_style: Protruding metallic handle, horizontally oriented, located on the upper section of the door.
attribute_vent_or_grille_pattern: Horizontal slats covering most of the rear surface, with a rectangular lower access panel.
